title: ems_mci_command
description: A chemical plant explosion generates 40+ casualties requiring integrated EMS command. Activate the MCI plan, coordinate mutual aid, manage triage sectors, and liaise with hospitals.

timeline.title: Explosion Reported
timeline.description: Dispatch reports an explosion at the Northgate Chemical Plant. Multiple callers report casualties and a visible chemical plume. First EMS unit is 4 minutes out.

timeline.title: First Units On Scene
timeline.description: First EMS supervisor arrives. Approximately 40 casualties visible in the parking lot. Several victims with chemical burns. Plant safety officer reports chlorine gas release.

timeline.title: MCI Declared
timeline.description: Incident commander declares an MCI. Triage sector established 200m upwind. 3 ambulances on scene, 6 more en route. HAZMAT team ETA 15 minutes.

timeline.title: Mutual Aid Activated
timeline.description: Two neighbouring EMS agencies request guidance on staging location and radio channel before committing resources. Hospital notifications are pending.

timeline.title: Hospital Notifications
timeline.description: Regional trauma centre can accept 8 critical patients. District General can accept 12 moderate patients. A third hospital reports limited capacity and requests guidance on diversion.

timeline.title: Supply Shortage
timeline.description: Treatment sector reports critically low antidote supply — only 4 doses of hydroxocobalamin remaining for potential cyanide exposure from fire by-products. 8 patients show signs of cyanide toxicity.

timeline.title: Scene Stabilization
timeline.description: HAZMAT team confirms source is contained. Last critical patient being loaded. Media presence increasing. Documentation and demobilization planning required.

node-1.title: MCI Plan Activation
node-1.situation: You are the EMS Incident Commander. You arrive at a chemical plant explosion with 40+ visible casualties and an ongoing chlorine gas release. Your two crew members are ready for orders.
node-1.question: What is your immediate first action?

options.label: A
options.text: Declare an MCI, establish incident command upwind and uphill, transmit a METHANE report to dispatch, and request HAZMAT, fire, and additional EMS units before approaching casualties.

options.label: B
options.text: Immediately enter the scene to begin triaging casualties and request help while treating patients.

options.label: C
options.text: Wait for HAZMAT to arrive and clear the scene before any EMS personnel approach casualties.

node-2.title: Triage Sector Establishment
node-2.situation: The scene is now established with an upwind command post. You have 40 casualties and 3 ambulances. Triage teams are ready. Multiple casualties are self-evacuating toward your position.
node-2.question: How do you structure the triage sector?

options.label: A
options.text: Designate a triage officer to direct START triage, establish colour-coded treatment areas (Red/Yellow/Green/Black), set a single entry point for ambulatory patients, and assign a treatment officer to each sector.

options.label: B
options.text: Have each ambulance crew triage independently and begin transport as patients are assessed.

options.label: C
options.text: Treat all ambulatory (Green) patients first since they can communicate and it's faster.

node-3.title: Hospital Liaison
node-3.situation: Triage complete: 9 Red, 14 Yellow, 15 Green, 2 Black. Three hospitals are awaiting your transport plan. Trauma centre can accept 8 critical, District General can accept 12 moderate, Community Hospital has limited capacity.
node-3.question: How do you coordinate hospital distribution?

options.label: A
options.text: Assign a medical communications officer to notify all three hospitals with patient counts and ETAs: send 8 Red patients to trauma centre, 12 Yellow patients to District General, and advise Community Hospital to stand by for Green patients.

options.label: B
options.text: Send all critical patients to the closest hospital regardless of capacity.

options.label: C
options.text: Let each ambulance crew choose which hospital to use based on their preference.

node-4.title: Mutual Aid Coordination
node-4.situation: Two neighbouring EMS agencies have arrived at the perimeter with 6 additional ambulances. They need a staging location, radio channel assignment, and task briefing before integration.
node-4.question: How do you integrate the mutual aid units?

options.label: A
options.text: Brief the incoming unit supervisors at the command post: assign them to the staging area 300m from triage, assign a common radio channel, and integrate them into the transport rotation under the transport officer's direction.

options.label: B
options.text: Tell mutual aid units to self-integrate and begin transporting whatever patients they can reach.

options.label: C
options.text: Refuse mutual aid units until your own crews have completed transport of all Red patients.

node-5.title: Supply Management
node-5.situation: Treatment sector reports only 4 doses of hydroxocobalamin remain, but 8 patients show signs of cyanide toxicity from fire by-products. Regional pharmacy can dispatch 6 additional doses in 45 minutes.
node-5.question: How do you manage the antidote shortage?

options.label: A
options.text: Request the regional emergency pharmaceutical dispatch immediately, triage the 8 suspected cyanide patients by severity, administer antidote to the most critical 4, and initiate supportive care for the remaining patients pending resupply.

options.label: B
options.text: Distribute the 4 doses equally among all 8 patients (half doses each).

options.label: C
options.text: Withhold all antidotes until confirmed lab results establish cyanide poisoning.

node-6.title: Public Information Officer Briefing
node-6.situation: Media have gathered at the perimeter. Social media posts with incorrect casualty counts are circulating. The plant's communications manager is asking to speak to press. Your PIO has arrived.
node-6.question: How do you manage public information?

options.label: A
options.text: Designate your PIO as the sole media contact, establish a press briefing point away from the command post, provide confirmed casualty counts and scene status only, and politely decline the plant's request to brief independently until a coordinated message is developed.

options.label: B
options.text: Allow the plant communications manager to brief the media directly without coordination.

options.label: C
options.text: Issue no public statement until all casualties are transported and the scene is cleared.

node-7.title: Documentation and Demobilization
node-7.situation: HAZMAT has confirmed the source is contained. The last critical patient is being loaded. You need to complete the incident record and begin structured demobilization before releasing mutual aid resources.
node-7.question: What are the final steps in your MCI command role?

options.label: A
options.text: Complete a full patient accounting against triage tags, record all decisions and timings in the incident log, conduct a hot debrief with sector officers, and release mutual aid units with written documentation of their contributions.

options.label: B
options.text: Release all resources immediately once the last ambulance departs and complete paperwork later.

node-8.title: Scenario Complete
node-8.situation: Excellent command performance. You successfully activated the MCI plan, established a structured incident command with designated sector officers, coordinated mutual aid integration, distributed 40+ patients across three hospitals based on acuity and capacity, managed a critical supply shortage, and ensured professional public information management. Your documentation will support a comprehensive HSEEP after-action review.
